display_name: Syslog severity level reflects the message class the developer assigned, not real-world impact
context: Triaging device logs and prioritizing events by their syslog severity number/keyword
observation: Log messages carry severity levels (0 emergency through 7 debug) and the engineer is using the level to decide what matters
naive_interpretation: Lower severity numbers are always the urgent problems and higher numbers are always safe to ignore
correct_interpretation: The severity is a static label the developer chose for a message class, not a measure of current operational impact. A level-5 notification such as BGP-5-ADJCHANGE marks a routing neighbor going down, which can be a major outage. Conversely, some level-3 errors are transient or cosmetic. Severity is useful for coarse filtering, but triage must read what the message actually says and correlate it with symptoms, not rank purely by the number.
why: Syslog severity (RFC 5424) is assigned per message at code-authoring time and does not adapt to the runtime context
Many high-impact state changes are logged as notifications (level 5) or informational (level 6) by convention, e.g. adjacency and link transitions
Some error-level (level 3) messages are recoverable or benign in the observed scenario, so the number overstates urgency
Effective triage correlates message content and timing with the reported symptom rather than sorting solely by severity

confirmation_commands.purpose: Read the actual message text and timestamps around the incident rather than filtering on severity alone
Surface high-impact state changes that are logged at notification/informational levels despite their importance

traps: Filtering out level 5-6 messages and missing the BGP/OSPF adjacency or link-down events that explain the outage
Escalating every level-3 error without reading whether it is transient or cosmetic in context
Sorting logs purely by severity instead of correlating message content with the reported symptom and timeline
Assuming severity is consistent across vendors — the same condition can be logged at different levels on different platforms
